title: canthium subcapitatum
intro: Canthium subcapitatum, also known as the "Wild Garlic", is a type of plant species that belongs to the family Rubiaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, Canthium subcapitatum is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
